LanguageData.MF_Setting_ShowDebugLogs_Tip: Enables detailed logs in the console (Dev Mode).
LanguageData.MF_OnlyExistingXenotypes_Desc: If enabled, new factions will only have xenotypes that are ALREADY present in at least one faction in the game world. Prevents the appearance of species that do not exist in your current playthrough.
LanguageData.MF_GenerateHybrids_Desc: If enabled, new factions will mutate upon splitting or appearing, creating their own unique xenotypes. If disabled, factions will use standard xenotype sets from their archetypes.
LanguageData.MF_Setting_EnableUIPatch_Tip: If enabled, the faction list will show an enhanced view with enemy icons and ideologies. Disable this if you have conflicts with other UI mods.
LanguageData.MF_Setting_AllowArchiteGenes_Tip: If enabled, mutations may randomly produce archite genes (requiring capsules). IMPORTANT: This makes factions significantly stronger.
LanguageData.MF_RequireCommsForNews_Tip: If enabled, notifications about faction appearances will only arrive if a comms console is present.
LanguageData.MF_Setting_RandomSettlementChance_Value: {0}% (Independent chance to spawn on an empty tile)
LanguageData.MF_Setting_SpawnOnRuinsChance_Value: {0}% (Independent chance to spawn specifically on ruin sites)
LanguageData.MF_Setting_ChanceTechAdvance_Tip: Chance that a breakaway faction will receive equipment and an archetype one tech-level HIGHER than the parent.
LanguageData.MF_Setting_ChanceTechRegress_Tip: Chance that a breakaway faction will lose knowledge and receive equipment one tech-level LOWER than the parent.
LanguageData.MF_Setting_PlayerRelationChances: Relationship chances with PLAYER on spawn:
LanguageData.MF_Setting_BaseTemplate_Desc: Allows the faction to use its own original spawn settings (empty xml defs without archetypes) on par with other mods.\n\nIMPORTANT: This may generate medieval factions that closely resemble industrial ones, as the vanilla game lacks strict classic medieval factions.
LanguageData.MF_HybridDescriptionBase: A unique lineage with obscure roots. Too many elements have mixed within these beings to judge their true origin with certainty.
LanguageData.MF_HybridDescriptionSingle: These beings clearly show traits of the {0} type at their core, yet significant changes have occurred that made their appearance and nature distinct.
LanguageData.MF_HybridDescriptionPartial: A mixed form combining features of different bloodlines. A clear kinship with the {0} type is evident, complemented by characteristic features of the {1} type.
LanguageData.MF_HybridDescriptionFull: A complex intertwining of many different traits. This appearance reveals features of the {0} and {1} types, as well as faint traces of other lineages: {2}.
LanguageData.MF_Setting_EnableFactionCulling_Tip: If a faction is completely destroyed, its xenotype and data will be automatically wiped from memory after a set number of days.\n\nThis returns the "dead" xenotype back into the free pool and prevents save bloat, but surviving members of this race will no longer appear in the world.
